listeners.purpose: Load the canonical token vocabulary from data/token-vocabulary.json into an in-memory registry used by PresetValidator + ThemeTokenOverrideObserver. First-touch cost paid at boot.
Rebuild the in-memory vocabulary registry on hot-reload.

New tenant provisioned → create a light Theme row referencing config('theme.presets.default_slug') (default: light-default) + activate it. Runs after commit on TenantProvisioned so a partial tenant creation never leaves an orphan theme row.

Cascade-delete every Theme, tenant-custom ThemePreset, and ThemeTokenOverride for the erased tenant. Platform presets survive. FK cascade handles the delete; this listener writes the compliance audit trail.
GDPR Art. 17 — delete the user's user_theme_preference row. The row lives in the user module; theme is the semantic owner + drives the compliance side.
Bust the theme:resolved:{tenant_id}:{mode} + theme:compiled-css:{tenant_id}:{mode} Redis keys. Runs synchronously — cache invalidation must land before the compiled CSS regen writes.

Run ContrastAuditor over the activated theme; if any color pair drops below WCAG AA, dispatch ThemeAccessibilityRecommendationNotification. Gated by config('theme.accessibility.audit_on_activate') + theme_accessibility_audit entitlement.
Dispatch RegenerateRenderedCssJob for the parent theme's tenant. Debounced via the job's shouldBeUnique+uniqueFor(60s) so a burst of overrides in one form-submit coalesces into a single rebuild.
Same debounced regen dispatch as ThemeTokenOverridden — override removal falls through to preset default, still needs a CSS rebuild.
